originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Big News from Djibouti: Our Leader Stays!
content:
Hello from Djibouti!

Hello, amazing friends! Are you ready for some exciting news from Africa? Today, we're flying over to a special country called Djibouti! It's a small but very sunny place in East Africa, right by the beautiful sea. People in Djibouti love their country and work hard together. Djibouti has lovely beaches and very interesting deserts. It is a place where different cultures meet and share stories. Imagine seeing colorful fish in the Red Sea near Djibouti! Did you know? Djibouti is famous for its super salty Lake Assal! It's one of the saltiest lakes in the whole world, even saltier than the ocean! Isn't that cool?

Meet President Guelleh!

Every country has a special person who helps lead it. This person is called the president. In Djibouti, the president is Mr. Ismail Omar Guelleh. He is a very important leader for his country. President Guelleh helps make big choices for all the people in Djibouti. He wants everyone to be safe and happy. He helps decide about schools, roads, and making sure there is enough food. A president is like the head of a big family, but for an entire country! It's a very big job. Did you know? The capital city of Djibouti is also called Djibouti City! It's a busy port city where many ships come and go.

What is an Election?

Sometimes, when it's time to choose a leader, people in a country have an election. An election is like a big vote. All the grown-ups get to choose who they want to be their president. They go to a special place and put a mark next to the name of the person they think will be the best leader. It's like choosing your favorite color, but for a whole country! When many people choose the same person, that person wins the election. It means the people trust that person to lead them. Did you know? Voting is a way for people to have their say. It's how grown-ups help decide important things for their country.

President Guelleh Won Again!

Recently, Djibouti had an election. Many people went to vote for their next president. And guess what? President Ismail Omar Guelleh won the election! He got almost all the votes! This means nearly everyone who voted chose him to be their leader again. It shows that many, many people in Djibouti believe he is doing a good job. When a president wins an election, it means they will continue to lead the country for some more years. It's like getting to play your favorite game again because you're so good at it! Did you know? A high percentage, like 97.8%, means almost everyone agreed! It's like if 97 out of 100 friends chose the same ice cream flavor!

He Has Led For a Long Time!

President Guelleh is 78 years old. That's older than most of your parents or even your grandparents! He has been the president of Djibouti for a very, very long time already. He has been the leader for 27 years! Imagine how many birthdays that is! If you are 7 years old, he has been president for almost four times your life! Because he won the election again, he will continue to be president for even more years. This will be his sixth time being chosen as president. He has seen many changes in Djibouti during all these years. He has helped his country grow and develop. Did you know? A president's job is not just for a day! It usually lasts for many years, called a 'term', before people vote again.

What Does a President Do?

A president has many important jobs. They work to make sure children can go to school and learn new things. They also help build hospitals so people can be healthy when they are sick. They also work hard to keep the country peaceful and safe. They talk to leaders from other countries to make sure everyone gets along. They want Djibouti to be a good place for everyone to live. President Guelleh has been working on these things for a long time. He wants to make Djibouti even better for all the families who live there. Did you know? Djibouti is very important for ships because of where it is on the map! Many ships stop there when they travel between different parts of the world.

Working Together for Djibouti!

Even though the president is the leader, everyone helps to make a country great. Children who study hard, parents who work, and neighbors who help each other all make Djibouti strong. The president guides everyone, just like a coach guides a team. Together, they can achieve amazing things for their country. They can build a bright future. We hope President Guelleh and all the people of Djibouti continue to work together for peace and happiness. It's wonderful when leaders and citizens work as a team!

Fun Facts About Djibouti!

Did you know? Djibouti is home to some amazing animals! You might see gazelles, ostriches, and even different kinds of sea turtles in the waters nearby! Another fun fact: Djibouti is known for its beautiful coral reefs! These are like underwater cities where many colorful fish and sea creatures live. They are very important for the ocean. And one more! The weather in Djibouti is usually very warm and sunny, almost all year round. Perfect for playing outside and enjoying the beautiful land! It's exciting to learn about our African neighbors and their leaders. We wish President Guelleh and all the wonderful people of Djibouti the very best!
summary: President Ismail Omar Guelleh has won the election in Djibouti with almost all the votes, meaning he will lead the country again after already being president for 27 years.
whyMatters: This is important because President Guelleh will continue to make big decisions for all the people in Djibouti, helping to build schools, keep everyone safe, and guide the country's future for more years.

originalTitle: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
title: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
content: UK government officials say they are not entirely abandoning the agreement but have run out of time.

originalTitle: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity Sentebale he co-founded
title: Prince Harry and a Special Club Have a Disagreement!
content:
Hello, Super Reporters!

Hello everyone! It’s your friendly African news reporter here, ready to tell you about something happening far away, but about someone who cares a lot about Africa. We're going to talk about a kind prince named Prince Harry and a special club called Sentebale. Sometimes, even good people and good clubs can have little disagreements, just like you might with your friends or family. It's okay, we're here to understand it together!

Imagine you have a club with your friends to help puppies or collect toys for other children. That’s kind of like what a "charity" is! A charity is a special group of people who work together to help other people who need it, without asking for money for themselves. They just want to make the world a better place. Prince Harry is a real prince from a country far away called the United Kingdom, and he loves to help people, especially children in Africa.

Who is Prince Harry?

Prince Harry is a grown-up prince. He’s very famous because he’s part of the Royal Family. That means his grandma was the Queen! Wow! Prince Harry has a kind heart. He grew up learning how important it is to help people who are not as lucky. He has visited many places in Africa and saw that some children needed extra help.

Did you know? Prince Harry visited Africa many times when he was younger. He really liked it and wanted to do something special to help children there. That’s how the idea for Sentebale started!

What is Sentebale?

Sentebale is a very special club or charity. Prince Harry helped start this club a long time ago with another kind person, Prince Seeiso from a country in Africa called Lesotho. Sentebale means "Forget Me Not" in a special language from Lesotho. Isn't that a lovely name? It means they don't want to forget the children who need help.

This club helps children and young people, especially those who might be sick or who need support to go to school and have a happy life. They work in countries like Lesotho and Botswana. These are beautiful countries in Africa, full of amazing people and nature.

Imagine if you were sick and needed help understanding your medicine, or if you needed a friend to talk to. That’s what Sentebale tries to do for many children. They help children learn, play, and feel strong and brave.

A Disagreement About Helping

Now, sometimes, even when people want to do good things, they can have disagreements. Prince Harry was one of the people who helped start Sentebale. He was like one of the main leaders of the club for a long, long time. But last year, Prince Harry decided to stop being a leader for Sentebale. He stepped away from helping to run the club.

Why did he stop? Well, the news says he had a big "dispute" or a "disagreement" about how the club was being "managed." Think of it like this: if you and your friends run a lemonade stand, and you disagree about how to sell the lemonade or how to share the money, that’s a disagreement about "management." Prince Harry had different ideas about how the club should be run, how it should help children, and how its money should be used.

Did you know? Running a big charity with lots of people helping can be very tricky. Everyone wants to do their best, but sometimes they have different ideas about the best way to do it!

What Does "Sued" Mean?

Here's the tricky part, but we can understand it! The news says that Sentebale, the club, is "suing" Prince Harry. What does that mean? It means they are taking him to a special grown-up place called a "court." In a court, there's a "judge" who is like a very smart teacher. The judge listens to both sides of a story and helps decide what is fair.

Sentebale is saying that Prince Harry said some things about the club that were not true, and these untrue things made the club look bad. When someone says untrue things about another person or a club that make them look bad, it's sometimes called "defamation." It's like if someone spread a rumor about you that wasn't true and made your friends think badly of you. Sentebale feels hurt by what they say Prince Harry said.

So, Sentebale wants the judge to help them. They want the judge to say that what Prince Harry said was not true, and maybe they want him to say sorry, or perhaps stop saying those things. It's like when you tell your teacher that a friend said something mean about you that wasn't true, and you want your teacher to help make things right.

Why Did Prince Harry Care So Much?

Prince Harry helped start Sentebale because he loves Africa very much. He spent a lot of time in a country called Lesotho, and he saw children who needed help. He saw children who didn't have their parents anymore, or who were sick and didn't have much support. He wanted to make sure these children were not forgotten.

He really wanted to make a difference. So, he and Prince Seeiso from Lesotho worked together to create this amazing club. They wanted to build schools, give support, and make sure children could grow up healthy and happy. It was a very good thing they did!

Did you know? Helping others makes you feel happy too! When you share your toys or help a friend, you feel a warm fuzzy feeling inside. That’s why many people, like Prince Harry, love to help!

Sentebale Still Helps Children

Even with this disagreement, it's very important to remember that Sentebale is still a very kind club. They are still working every single day to help children and young people in Africa. They are still making sure children get the support they need to learn, to stay healthy, and to have bright futures. Their work is super important!

Disagreements can happen, but the good work of helping others often continues. The children in Lesotho and Botswana are still getting help from Sentebale, and that is a wonderful thing. This disagreement is between the grown-ups who used to work together, not about the children they help.

So, even though there's a disagreement, the main thing is that a good club is still doing good work. We hope that everything will be sorted out in a fair way, and that Sentebale can continue its important mission of helping children across Africa. Thank you for being such great listeners, super reporters!
summary: Prince Harry, who helped start a special club called Sentebale that helps children in Africa, is now in a disagreement with the club because they say he said things that were not true about how they run things.
whyMatters: This matters because even good people who want to help can have disagreements, and it reminds us that special clubs like Sentebale still work hard every day to help children in Africa.

originalTitle: Artemis II crew seen on recovery ship after moon mission return
title: Our Super Space Friends are Ready for the Moon!
content:
Hello, Super Space Heroes!

Welcome, young explorers, to some amazing news from your kid-friendly reporter! Today we are talking about very brave people. They are called astronauts. Astronauts are like super explorers who travel far, far away. They go on big adventures in space. These friends are getting ready for a very special trip. It is a trip all the way to the Moon! Wow!

What is Artemis II?

Artemis II is a very special name. It is the name for a super important space mission. A mission is like a big task or journey. This mission will take brave astronauts into space. They will fly very far from Earth. Their goal is to fly around our friend, the Moon. They will not land on the Moon this time. But they will fly very, very close to it! This trip helps us learn more about space. It helps us get ready for bigger trips later. Did you know? The Moon is Earth's only natural friend in space! It looks small from here, but it is actually very, very big. The Moon helps us by making the ocean water go up and down every day. This is called the tide.

Meet Our Astronaut Friends!

Who are these amazing people going to the Moon? They are super smart and very brave. Their names are Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. Three are from a country called America, and one is from Canada. They train super hard every day. Training means learning and practicing a lot. They learn how to live in a spaceship. They learn how to fly it safely. They also learn how to eat and sleep in space! It's very different from Earth because there is no gravity.

Coming Home from Space

Imagine going on a very long journey far away. Then, it's time to come back home to Earth. Coming home from space is very exciting, but also very tricky. The spaceship comes zooming down from the sky! It does not land on a runway like an aeroplane. Instead, it lands in the big, big ocean! SPLASH! This is called a "splashdown." Did you know? Spaceships use giant parachutes to slow down before they splash in the water. These parachutes are much, much bigger than the ones people use to jump from aeroplanes!

A Special Ship to Help

When the spaceship splashes down in the ocean, it needs help. A special kind of ship waits for it. This ship is called a "recovery ship." It's like a big rescue boat, but it's even more special. Its job is to find the spaceship and the astronauts. Then, it helps them get out of the spaceship safely. It makes sure our brave space explorers are safe and sound after their long journey.

What Happened Recently?

Recently, our astronaut friends were seen on this big recovery ship. They were getting help to walk across its deck. But guess what? They were not actually coming back from the Moon yet! This was a very, very important practice. It was like a big dress rehearsal! They were practicing how to come home safely. They wore special orange suits. These suits help them float in the water and stay safe. They learn what it feels like to get out of the spaceship onto the ship. This helps them be ready for the real thing. It helps them prepare their bodies for being back on Earth. Did you know? Astronauts sometimes feel a bit wobbly or dizzy when they first come back from space. This is because their bodies get used to floating where there is no gravity! On Earth, gravity pulls us down and keeps our feet on the ground.

Why is Practice Important?

Practicing is super important for everything we do. It's like practicing your ABCs or practicing how to kick a ball for football. Astronauts practice all the time. They practice for every part of their trip. They practice flying, living in space, and especially coming back to Earth. This helps them become super good at their job. It also helps keep them safe. Safety is always the most important thing for these big space adventures! By practicing, they can fix any problems before the real trip. It makes sure everything goes perfectly when they finally fly around the Moon.

The Moon, Our Big Neighbour!

The Moon is a wonderful and mysterious place. We can see it shining bright in our night sky, almost every night. It is very, very far away from Earth. But people have actually visited it before, many years ago! Artemis II is part of a new plan. This plan is to go back to the Moon. And not just to visit, but to learn even more. One day, people might even build places to live on the Moon! And after that, maybe even fly to the planet Mars! Did you know? The Moon does not make its own light, even though it looks so bright! It actually reflects light from the Sun, like a giant mirror in space. That's why it looks like it's glowing!

Dreams from Africa to the Stars!

We can all look up at the night sky from our homes in Africa. We can see the same beautiful Moon and twinkling stars that astronauts see. Maybe one day, a brave young boy or girl from Africa will become an astronaut. Maybe YOU will be the one to go to the Moon! Keep learning, keep dreaming big, and keep exploring the world around you. These brave astronauts show us that even the biggest dreams can come true. We are all so excited to cheer them on when they finally fly around the Moon! Let's dream of reaching for the stars together!
summary: Brave astronauts are practicing how to return to Earth safely after their upcoming trip around the Moon, getting ready on a special ship called a recovery ship.
whyMatters: This practice is very important because it makes sure the astronauts stay safe when they come back from their big trip around the Moon, and it helps us learn more about going to space in the future.
